title: Plan a zero-downtime schema migration
description: Sequence an expand-migrate-contract rollout so reads and writes never see a missing or half-populated column.
body: Use when a schema change must ship without a maintenance window. Actions: expand by adding the new nullable column or table with online DDL; backfill in bounded batches with a sleep between chunks to spare the binlog and replicas; dual-write from the application so old and new shapes stay consistent; cut reads over once backfill verification passes; then contract by dropping the old column in a later release. Verify each phase with row-count reconciliation between old and new shapes. Validation signals include replica lag staying bounded during backfill and a reconciliation query returning zero mismatches. Risk boundary: the contract step is irreversible, so keep the old column until a full deploy cycle proves the new path and a human signs off on the drop.
